| `basisVec_expectation_totalSpinHalfOp1` / `_totalSpinHalfOp2` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse `Ňú_tot^(1,2)` zero expectation on every basis state**: `<basisVec ŌÉ \| Ňú_tot^(1) \| basisVec ŌÉ> = 0` and `<basisVec ŌÉ \| Ňú_tot^(2) \| basisVec ŌÉ> = 0`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ő≥-4 step 214; per-site uses `basisVec_expectation_eq_diagonal` + `pauliX/Y` zero diagonal (ő≥-4 step 215) | `Quantum/SpinDot/Hamiltonian.lean` (PR #1262) |
| `basisVec_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_general` | **Spin-`1/2` `(Ňú_tot)¬≤` Casimir expectation on arbitrary `basisVec ŌÉ`**: `<basisVec ŌÉ \| (Ňú_tot)¬≤ \| basisVec ŌÉ> = \|őõ\|/2 + (M(ŌÉ)/2)¬≤` for any `ŌÉ : őõ ‚Üí Fin 2`. Generalises ő≥-4 step 201 (constant case has `M = ¬Ī\|őõ\|` giving `\|őõ\|¬∑(\|őõ\|+2)/4`). Proof: reduce to diagonal matrix element, expand `(Ňú_tot)¬≤ = ő£_{x,y} spinHalfDot x y`, unify per-pair diagonal as `m_x m_y/4 + (1/2)¬∑[x=y]`, then split via separability (ő≥-4 step 216) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1263) |
| `basisVecS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` `(Ňú_tot)¬≤` Casimir expectation on arbitrary `basisVecS ŌÉ`**: `<basisVecS ŌÉ \| (Ňú_tot)¬≤ \| basisVecS ŌÉ> = \|V\|¬∑N(N+2)/4 + magEigenvalueS(ŌÉ)¬≤ ‚ąí ő£_x (N/2 ‚ąí ŌÉx.val)¬≤` for any `ŌÉ : őõ ‚Üí Fin (N + 1)`. Spin-`S` analogue of ő≥-4 step 216; the per-site sum-of-squares term is the configuration-dependent z-axis squared contribution that doesn't collapse for spin-`S`. Spin-`1/2` is the special case where the term reduces to constant `\|őõ\|/4` (ő≥-4 step 218) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1265) |
| `basisVec_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir `(Ňú_tot^(1))¬≤ + (Ňú_tot^(2))¬≤` on arbitrary `basisVec ŌÉ` is constant `\|őõ\|/2`**: independent of `ŌÉ`. Direct corollary of ő≥-4 step 216 + ő≥-4 step 204 (z-axis cancels). Expresses the per-state transverse contribution as a pure combinatorial quantity (ő≥-4 step 219) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1266) |
| `basisVecS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(Ňú_tot^(1))¬≤ + (Ňú_tot^(2))¬≤` on arbitrary `basisVecS ŌÉ` = `\|V\|¬∑N(N+2)/4 ‚ąí ő£_x (N/2 ‚ąí ŌÉx.val)¬≤`**. Direct corollary of ő≥-4 step 218 + ő≥-4 step 205 (`magEigenvalueS¬≤` cancels). Reduces to `\|V\|¬∑N/2` for N√©el on balanced bipartite graphs (matches ő≥-4 step 156) and to `\|őõ\|/2` for spin-`1/2` (ő≥-4 step 220) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1267) |
| `neelConfigOfS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **N√©el per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `ő£_x (N/2 ‚ąí (neelConfigOfS A N x).val)¬≤ = \|V\|¬∑(N/2)¬≤`. Both extremes `ŌÉx ‚ąą {0, N}` give `(N/2)¬≤`. Bridges ő≥-4 step 218 (general formula) to existing closed-form N√©el Casimir (ő≥-4 step 221) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1268) |
| `allAlignedConfigS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **AllAligned per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `ő£_x (N/2 ‚ąí (allAlignedConfigS V N c x).val)¬≤ = \|V\|¬∑(N/2 ‚ąí c.val)¬≤`. Trivial direct computation since `ŌÉx = c` constant. Bridges ő≥-4 step 218 to existing `allAlignedStateS_zero_/_last_expectation_totalSpinSSquared` (ő≥-4 step 222) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` (PR #1269) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS` | **N√©el `Ňú_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N) = (\|A\| ‚ąí \|¬¨A\|)¬∑N/2`. Direct from `magSumS_neelConfigOfS` (= `\|¬¨A\|¬∑N`) and the filter-card decomposition `\|V\| = \|A\| + \|¬¨A\|`. Named lemma extracted from inlined computation in `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_card_Lambda` (ő≥-4 step 224) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelBasisVecS.lean` (PR #1271) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`S` N√©el Casimir re-derived via the general formula**: `<ő¶_N√©el \| (Ňú_tot)¬≤ \| ő¶_N√©el> = ((\|A\|‚ąí\|¬¨A\|)¬∑N/2)¬≤ + \|V\|¬∑N/2` derived from ő≥-4 step 218 + ő≥-4 step 221 + ő≥-4 step 224 + `ring`. Unification check against the existing `_card_Lambda` form (ő≥-4 step 127). Validates that the general formula correctly specializes to the N√©el-specific result (ő≥-4 step 225) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelBasisVecS.lean` (PR #1272) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero/_last_totalSpinSSquared_via_general` | **Spin-`S` allAligned-up/down Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<ő¶_‚ä§ \| (Ňú_tot)¬≤ \| ő¶_‚ä§> = m_max¬∑(m_max+1)` (and same for `ő¶_‚ä•`) where `m_max = \|V\|¬∑N/2`. Composition of ő≥-4 step 218 + ő≥-4 step 222 + `magEigenvalueS_allAlignedConfigS` + `ring`. Recovers existing `allAlignedStateS_zero_expectation_totalSpinSSquared` (ő≥-4 step 226) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1273) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` N√©el Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<ő¶_N√©el \| (Ňú_tot)¬≤ \| ő¶_N√©el> = ((\|A\|‚ąí\|¬¨A\|)/2)¬≤ + \|őõ\|/2`. Composition of ő≥-4 step 216 + `magnetization_neelConfigOf` + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ő≥-4 step 225 (ő≥-4 step 227) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1274) |
| `basisVec_const_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` const-state Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<basisVec(const s) \| (Ňú_tot)¬≤ \| basisVec(const s)> = \|őõ\|¬∑(\|őõ\|+2)/4` for any `s : Fin 2`. Composition of ő≥-4 step 216 + `magnetization (const s) = ¬Ī\|őõ\|` + `(spinSign s)¬≤ = 1` + `ring`. Recovers existing ő≥-4 step 201 (ő≥-4 step 228) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1275) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement` | **Complement N√©el `Ňú_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (¬¨A) N) = (\|¬¨A\|‚ąí\|A\|)¬∑N/2`. Sublattice-swap symmetry; complement of ő≥-4 step 224. Direct corollary by applying ő≥-4 step 224 to `(¬¨A)` (ő≥-4 step 229) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1276) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_simplified` | **Cleaner form** of ő≥-4 step 229 with double negation `!!A` simplified to `A` via `Finset.filter_congr` + `Bool.not_not`: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (¬¨A) N) = (\|¬¨A\|‚ąí\|A\|)¬∑N/2` (ő≥-4 step 230) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1277) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_eq_neg` | **Negation relation**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (¬¨A) N) = ‚ąímagEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N)`. Direct from ő≥-4 step 224 + ő≥-4 step 230 + `ring`. The complement N√©el sits at the opposite `Ňú_tot^(3)` eigenvalue (ő≥-4 step 231) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1278) |
| `magnetization_neelConfigOf_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` negation relation**: `magnetization őõ (neelConfigOf (¬¨A)) = ‚ąímagnetization őõ (neelConfigOf A)`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ő≥-4 step 231; direct from existing `magnetization_neelConfigOf{,_complement}` + `ring` (ő≥-4 step 232) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1279) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: `<ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|(Ňú_tot)¬≤\|ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = <ő¶_N√©el(A)\|(Ňú_tot)¬≤\|ő¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from ő≥-4 step 227 (Casimir = `(M/2)¬≤+\|őõ\|/2`) + ő≥-4 step 232 (`M(¬¨A) = ‚ąíM(A)`): `(M)¬≤` is sign-flip invariant (ő≥-4 step 233) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1280) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: `<ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|(Ňú_tot)¬≤\|ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = <ő¶_N√©el(A)\|(Ňú_tot)¬≤\|ő¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from ő≥-4 step 218 + 221 + 231 + `ring`: `magEigenvalueS¬≤` is sign-flip invariant; `ő£_x m_x¬≤` evaluates to `\|V\|¬∑(N/2)¬≤` for both A and ¬¨A. Spin-`S` mirror of ő≥-4 step 233 (ő≥-4 step 234) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1281) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` `(Ňú_tot^(3))¬≤` is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: `<ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|(Ňú_tot^(3))¬≤\|ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = <ő¶_N√©el(A)\|(Ňú_tot^(3))¬≤\|ő¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from ő≥-4 step 205 + 231 + `ring`: squared eigenvalue is sign-flip invariant (ő≥-4 step 235) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1282) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` `(Ňú_tot^(3))¬≤` is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: `<ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|(Ňú_tot^(3))¬≤\|ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = <ő¶_N√©el(A)\|(Ňú_tot^(3))¬≤\|ő¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from ő≥-4 step 204 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ő≥-4 step 235 (ő≥-4 step 236) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1283) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(Ňú^1)¬≤+(Ňú^2)¬≤` is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: equal expectations on `ő¶_N√©el(A)` and `ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)`. Both reduce to `\|V\|¬∑N/2` via ő≥-4 step 220 + 221 (ő≥-4 step 237) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1284) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on N√©el**: both expectations equal `\|őõ\|/2`. Direct from ő≥-4 step 219 (ŌÉ-independent constant). Spin-`1/2` mirror of ő≥-4 step 237 (ő≥-4 step 238) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1285) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `Ňú_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N√©el**: `<ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|Ňú_tot^(3)\|ő¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = ‚ąí<ő¶_N√©el(A)\|Ňú_tot^(3)\|ő¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from ő≥-4 step 206 + 231 (ő≥-4 step 239) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1286) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` linear `Ňú_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N√©el**. Direct from ő≥-4 step 207 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ő≥-4 step 239 (ő≥-4 step 240) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1287) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` N√©el transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<ő¶_N√©el\|Ňú_tot^(1,2)\|ő¶_N√©el> = 0`. Trivial corollary of ő≥-4 step 214 applied to `ŌÉ = neelConfigOfS A N` (ő≥-4 step 241). Together with ő≥-4 step 91 (`<Ňú_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|‚ąí\|¬¨A\|)¬∑N/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` N√©el transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as ő≥-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from ő≥-4 step 215 (ő≥-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = ő£_{j=1}^z Ňú_0 ¬∑ Ňú_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `‚ąíS(1 + zS)` (ő≥-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j ‚ąą univ.erase 0` (ő≥-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = ‚ąÖ` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `‚ąíS(1 + zS)` is intended for `z ‚Č• 1` (ő≥-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<ő¶_‚ä§\|H\|ő¶_‚ä§> = z¬∑(N/2)¬≤` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `‚ąíS(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (ő≥-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<ő¶_aligned(c)\|H\|ő¶_aligned(c)> = z¬∑(N/2 ‚ąí c.val)¬≤`. Generalises ő≥-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `z¬∑(N/2)¬≤` (ő≥-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<ő¶_‚ä•\|H\|ő¶_‚ä•> = z¬∑(N/2)¬≤`. Direct specialisation of ő≥-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (ő≥-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `Ňú_R^(őĪ) = ő£_{j ‚Č† 0} onSiteS j Ňú^(őĪ)` for axes `őĪ = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `Ňú^(őĪ)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (Ňú_tot¬≤ ‚ąí Ňú_0¬≤ ‚ąí Ňú_R¬≤)/2` (ő≥-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`Ňú_0 ¬∑ Ňú_R` decomposition**: `H = ő£_őĪ onSiteS 0 (Ňú^(őĪ)) ¬∑ leafSpinSOp_őĪ`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `ő£_j (A * B_j) = A * (ő£_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (ő≥-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_őĪ (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (Ňú^(őĪ)) + leafSpinSOp_őĪ z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (ő≥-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1298) |
| `leafSpinSSquared` | **Leaf-spin Casimir**: `Ňú_R¬≤ := (Ňú_R^(1))¬≤ + (Ňú_R^(2))¬≤ + (Ňú_R^(3))¬≤`, total-spin-squared restricted to leaves of `Fin (z+1)` (ő≥-5 step 252) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1299) |
| `onSiteS_zero_commute_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Center-leaf commutativity**: `Commute (onSiteS 0 Ňú^(őĪ)) (leafSpinSOp_őĪ z N)`. Direct from `Commute.sum_right` + `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (disjoint sites) (ő≥-5 step 253) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1300) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir decomposition**: `2¬∑H = (Ňú_tot)¬≤ ‚ąí Ňú_0¬≤ ‚ąí Ňú_R¬≤` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian, where `Ňú_0¬≤ = spinSDot 0 0 N` and `Ňú_R¬≤ = leafSpinSSquared z N`. Proof: expand `ő£_őĪ totalSpinSOp_őĪ¬≤ = ő£_őĪ (onSite 0 + leaf_őĪ)¬≤` via ő≥-5 step 251 + `(a+b)¬≤ = a¬≤ + 2ab + b¬≤` (using ő≥-5 step 253 commutativity) + ő≥-5 step 250 (cross term identification) + `noncomm_ring` (ő≥-5 step 254) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1301) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_smul_eq_casimir_diff` | **‚Ąā-smul Casimir form**: `(2 : ‚Ąā) ‚ÄĘ H = (Ňú_tot)¬≤ ‚ąí Ňú_0¬≤ ‚ąí Ňú_R¬≤`. Direct corollary of ő≥-5 step 254 via `two_mul + two_smul` (ő≥-5 step 255) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1302) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation` | **Casimir decomposition expectation form**: `2 ¬∑ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot¬≤\|v> ‚ąí <v\|S0¬≤\|v> ‚ąí <v\|SR¬≤\|v>` for any `v`. Direct corollary of ő≥-5 step 255 + linearity of `dotProduct + mulVec` over `smul` and subtraction (ő≥-5 step 256) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1303) |
| `spinSDot_self_expectation_general` | **Single-site Casimir expectation**: `<v\|spinSDot x x N\|v> = (N(N+2)/4) ¬∑ <v\|v>` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix) + linearity. Used to evaluate the `S0¬≤` term in the Casimir decomposition (ő≥-5 step 257) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1304) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation_simplified` | **Simplified Casimir expectation**: `2 ¬∑ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot¬≤\|v> ‚ąí (N(N+2)/4)¬∑<v\|v> ‚ąí <v\|SR¬≤\|v>`. Combines ő≥-5 step 256 + 257; the `S0¬≤` term is now an explicit scalar multiple of the norm-squared (ő≥-5 step 258) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1305) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_of_joint_casimir_eigenvec` | **H eigenvalue from joint Casimir eigenvalues**: if `Stot¬≤¬∑v = őĪ¬∑v` and `SR¬≤¬∑v = ő≤¬∑v`, then `H¬∑v = (őĪ ‚ąí N(N+2)/4 ‚ąí ő≤)/2 ¬∑ v`. Reduces the GS-energy problem to enumerating admissible `(őĪ, ő≤)` joint eigenvalues (ő≥-5 step 259) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1306) |
| `spinSDot_self_mulVec_eq_smul` | **Single-site Casimir as scalar action**: `spinSDot x x N ¬∑ v = (N(N+2)/4) ‚ÄĘ v` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix). Standalone helper extracted from ő≥-5 step 259 (ő≥-5 step 260) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1307) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3_zero_z` and `leafSpinSSquared_zero_z` | **Edge case `z=0`** (single vertex, no leaves): `leafSpinSOp_őĪ 0 N = 0` and `leafSpinSSquared 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = ‚ąÖ` in `Fin 1` (ő≥-5 step 261) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1308) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` | **`leafSpinSSquared` as double sum**: `leafSpinSSquared z N = ő£_{j,k ‚ąą univ.erase 0} spinSDot j k N`. Mirrors `totalSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` for the leaf-only Casimir (ő≥-5 step 262) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1309) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<ő¶_‚ä§\|leafSpinSSquared z N\|ő¶_‚ä§> = (zN/2)¬∑(zN/2 + 1) = s_R(s_R+1)` where `s_R = z¬∑(N/2)` is max leaf spin. | `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_commute_of_commute` | Tasaki ¬ß2.2 (2.2.12) ‚Üí (2.2.13): `Commute A (Ňú_tot^(őĪ)) ‚Üí Commute A (√õ^(őĪ)_őł_tot)`. Generic operator version, follows from `Commute.exp_right` after rewriting `√õ` via `_eq_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}_exp_commute_of_commute` | ladder version: `Commute A (Ňú^¬Ī_tot) ‚Üí Commute A (exp(c ‚ÄĘ Ňú^¬Ī_tot))` for any `c : ‚Ąā` (useful for U(1) symmetry) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conjTranspose_mul_self` | `(√õ^(őĪ)_őł_tot)Šīī * √õ^(őĪ)_őł_tot = 1` (unitarity). Derived from `exp_mem_unitary_of_mem_skewAdjoint` after recognizing `-iőł Ňú_tot^(őĪ)` as skew-adjoint | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_eq_self_of_commute` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.13) finite form: `Commute A (Ňú_tot^(őĪ)) ‚Üí (√õ^(őĪ)_őł_tot)Šīī * A * √õ^(őĪ)_őł_tot = A`. Combines `_commute_of_commute` with unitarity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `IsInMagnetizationSubspace` | predicate for the magnetization-`M` eigenspace `H_M` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace M` | the magnetization-`M` eigenspace as a `Submodule ‚Ąā ((őõ ‚Üí Fin 2) ‚Üí ‚Ąā)` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `basisVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace` | `|ŌÉ‚ü© ‚ąą H_{|ŌÉ|/2}` ‚ÄĒ basis states lie in their magnetization subspace | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_disjoint` | distinct sectors `H_M ‚äď H_{M'} = ‚ä•` (`M ‚Č† M'`) ‚ÄĒ eigenvalue uniqueness | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `iSup_magnetizationSubspace_eq_top` | `‚®Ü_M H_M = ‚ä§` ‚ÄĒ every vector decomposes as a sum across sectors | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_eq_eigenspace` | bridge `H_M = (Ňú_tot^(3) as End).eigenspace M` (used to inherit `iSupIndep`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_iSupIndep` | `iSupIndep`: each sector is disjoint from the supremum of all others | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_isInternal` | `DirectSum.IsInternal`: full direct-sum decomposition `H = ‚äē_M H_M` (Tasaki eqs. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |

### Two-site spin inner product (Tasaki ¬ß2.2 eq. (2.2.16))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.2 eq. (2.2.16), p. 24.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinHalfDot x y` | `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y := ő£_{őĪ} onSite x Ňú^(őĪ) ¬∑ onSite y Ňú^(őĪ)` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_eq_plus_minus` | `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y = (1/2)(Ňú_x^+ Ňú_y^- + Ňú_x^- Ňú_y^+) + Ňú_x^(3) Ňú_y^(3)` (Tasaki (2.2.16)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_comm` | `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y = Ňú_y ¬∑ Ňú_x` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_self` | `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_x = (3/4) ¬∑ 1` (the S(S+1) = 3/4 identity for S = 1/2) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_isHermitian` | `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` | `(Ňú_tot)¬≤ = ő£_{x,y} Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfPairSpinSq` / `spinHalfPairSpinSq_eq` | `(Ňú_x + Ňú_y)¬≤ = 2¬∑(Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y) + Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_x + Ňú_y ¬∑ Ňú_y` (Tasaki (2.2.18)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y, Ňú_tot^(őĪ)] = 0` for őĪ ‚ąą {1, 2, 3} (SU(2) invariance, Tasaki (2.2.17)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus` | `[Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y, Ňú^¬Ī_tot] = 0` (ladder-operator version of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_parallel` | `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y |ŌÉ‚ü© = (1/4) |ŌÉ‚ü©` when `ŌÉ x = ŌÉ y` and `x ‚Č† y` (Tasaki (2.2.19) parallel case) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_both_up/down` | `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y |‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ‚ü© = (1/4) |‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ‚ü©`, `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y |‚Üď‚Üď‚ü© = (1/4) |‚Üď‚Üď‚ü©` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = ¬Ī1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `basisSwap` / `basisSwap_involutive` / `basisSwap_antiparallel` | site-swap of `ŌÉ` at `x, y`, involutive and preserves anti-parallelism | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_antiparallel` | `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y |ŌÉ‚ü© = (1/2) |swap ŌÉ‚ü© - (1/4) |ŌÉ‚ü©` when `ŌÉ x ‚Č† ŌÉ y` (anti-parallel case) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_singlet` | singlet eigenvalue `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y (|ŌÉ‚ü© - |swap ŌÉ‚ü©) = -(3/4) (|ŌÉ‚ü© - |swap ŌÉ‚ü©)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) singlet `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_triplet_anti` | triplet `m = 0` eigenvalue `Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y (|ŌÉ‚ü© + |swap ŌÉ‚ü©) = (1/4) (|ŌÉ‚ü© + |swap ŌÉ‚ü©)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian` | `H = ő£_{x,y} J(x,y) Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y` (general Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[H, Ňú_tot^(őĪ)] = 0` for all axes (Tasaki (2.2.13) SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}` | `[H, Ňú^¬Ī_tot] = 0` (ladder form of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `Commute H Ňútot¬≤` ‚ÄĒ the Casimir operator-level form of SU(2) invariance (consequence of `[H, Ňútot^őĪ] = 0` for each őĪ, via `Commute.mul_right` and `.add_right`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_preserves_totalSpinHalfSquared_eigenvalue` | if `Ňútot¬≤ ¬∑ v = S ¬∑ v` then `Ňútot¬≤ ¬∑ (H ¬∑ v) = S ¬∑ (H ¬∑ v)` ‚ÄĒ operator-level simultaneous diagonalisation of `H` and the SU(2) Casimir | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_pauliZ` / `pauliZ_mul_spinHalfOpPlus` | `ŌÉ^+ ¬∑ ŌÉ^z = -ŌÉ^+` and `ŌÉ^z ¬∑ ŌÉ^+ = ŌÉ^+` ‚ÄĒ the (anti)commutation at the single-site Pauli algebra level, used for the Jordan-Wigner cross-site CAR | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `Ňú_tot¬≤ |s s ‚Ä¶ s‚ü© = (N(N+2)/4) |s s ‚Ä¶ s‚ü©` for any constant `s : Fin 2` (Casimir eigenvalue at maximum total spin `S = N/2`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specializations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOp{Minus,Plus}_pow_basisVec_const` | for any `s : Fin 2` and `k : ‚Ąē`, `Ňútot¬≤ ¬∑ (Ňútot^‚ąď)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü© = (|őõ|¬∑(|őõ|+2)/4) ¬∑ (Ňútot^‚ąď)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ the iterated ladder iterates remain in the maximum-total-spin SU(2) representation `S = Smax = |őõ|/2` (Casimir invariance, Tasaki ¬ß2.4) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `H |s‚Ä¶s‚ü© = (ő£_{x,y} J(x,y)¬∑(if x=y then 3/4 else 1/4)) ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ every Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian acts on a uniformly-aligned basis state as a scalar; bilinear-sum lift of Tasaki ¬ß2.4 eq. (2.4.5), p. 32 (`-Ňú_x¬∑Ňú_y |ő¶‚ÜĎ‚ü© = -S¬≤ |ő¶‚ÜĎ‚ü©` for `S = 1/2`, `x ‚Č† y`), with the diagonal `S(S+1) = 3/4` contribution recorded explicitly | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specialisations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) / `s = 1` (all-down) ‚ÄĒ the eigenvector property of the fully-polarised states; ground-state status (Tasaki's `E_GS = -|B|¬∑S¬≤`) requires extra ferromagnetic structure on `J` and is not asserted here | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_basisVec_const` | `H ¬∑ (Ňú_tot^+ ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ (Ňú_tot^+ ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)` ‚ÄĒ `Ňú_tot^+` preserves the H-eigenvalue on a constant-spin basis state (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_basisVec_const` | same with `Ňú_tot^-` ‚ÄĒ the canonical lowering ladder Tasaki uses to enumerate the ferromagnetic ground states `|ő¶_M‚ü©` (eq. (2.4.9), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_const` | iterated form: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : ‚Ąē`, `H ¬∑ ((Ňú_tot^-)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ ((Ňú_tot^-)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)`; specialised at `s = 0` this gives the unnormalised Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.9), p. 33 ‚ÄĒ every iterate `(Ňú_tot^-)^k ¬∑ |ő¶‚ÜĎ‚ü©` lies in the same H-eigenspace as `|ő¶‚ÜĎ‚ü©` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_const` | companion iterated form for `Ňú_tot^+`: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : ‚Ąē`, `H ¬∑ ((Ňú_tot^+)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ ((Ňú_tot^+)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)` (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), iterated) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}` | for any `J` and `őł : ‚ĄĚ`, `H` commutes with the global rotation `√õ^(őĪ)_őł = exp(-iőł Ňú_tot^őĪ)` (composes `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{őĪ}` with `totalSpinHalfRot{őĪ}_commute_of_commute`; the operator-level form of Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `őł`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ¬∑ (√õ^(őĪ)_őł ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ (√õ^(őĪ)_őł ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)` ‚ÄĒ the rotated (single-axis) constant-spin state shares the H-eigenvalue (Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot32_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `őł`, `Ōē`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ¬∑ (√õ^(3)_Ōē ¬∑ √õ^(2)_őł ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ (√õ^(3)_Ōē ¬∑ √õ^(2)_őł ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)` ‚ÄĒ the two-step spin-coherent state of Tasaki eq. (2.4.6) (`|őě_őł,Ōē‚ü©` for `s = 0`) is an H-eigenvector with the same eigenvalue as the constant configuration (Tasaki eq. (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | `Ňú_tot¬≤ (|‚ÜĎ‚Üď‚ü© - |‚Üď‚ÜĎ‚ü©) = 0` for `őõ = Fin 2` (singlet, `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_triplet_zero` | `Ňú_tot¬≤ (|‚ÜĎ‚Üď‚ü© + |‚Üď‚ÜĎ‚ü©) = 2(|‚ÜĎ‚Üď‚ü© + |‚Üď‚ÜĎ‚ü©)` for `őõ = Fin 2` (triplet `m = 0`, `S = 1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | the two-site singlet has zero `Ňú_tot^(3)` magnetization | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_totalOnSite` | `[onSite x SőĪ, ő£_z onSite z Ső≤] = onSite x [SőĪ, Ső≤]` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `ŌÉ = 2¬∑S` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J ő£ ŌÉ^z_i ŌÉ^z_{i+1} - h ő£ ŌÉ^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `ŌÉ^z_x ¬∑ ŌÉ^z_y` (generic in `őõ`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : őõ ‚Üí őõ ‚Üí ‚Ąā) (h : ‚Ąā)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `ő£ J(x,y) ŌÉ^z_x ŌÉ^z_y ‚ąí h ő£ ŌÉ^x_x` for any finite `őõ` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G ő≤ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `ő£_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `ő£_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i ‚Ü¶ i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the N√©el state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ¬ß2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 ‚Č° 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

| Lean name / location | Purpose |

| `LatticeSystem/Tests/Sanity.lean` | sanity-check `example` blocks for concrete small-N instances (Pauli arithmetic, spin-1/2 ladder actions, Heisenberg / Ising Hermiticity on small chains, graph-centric bridge identities) |
| decide-based property tests (in `Tests/Sanity.lean`) | universally-quantified properties verified by `decide` on small finite types (graph adjacency symmetry / irreflexivity / connectivity on `pathGraph n` and `cycleGraph n` for small `n`); real proofs, no `sorry` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState ő≤ J h N` | `gibbsState ő≤ (quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h)` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

| `quantumIsingGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[ŌĀ_ő≤, H_Ising] = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_zero` | infinite-temperature closed form `‚ü®A‚ü©_0 = (1/dim) ¬∑ Tr A` (independent of `J, h`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `O`, `(‚ü®O‚ü©_ő≤).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | conservation `‚ü®[H_Ising, A]‚ü©_ő≤ = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(‚ü®H_Ising‚ü©_ő≤).im = 0` (energy expectation is real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(‚ü®H_Ising ¬∑ O‚ü©_ő≤).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(‚ü®H_Ising^2‚ü©_ő≤).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(‚ü®H_Ising^n‚ü©_ő≤).im = 0` for any `n : ‚Ąē` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(‚ü®A¬∑B + B¬∑A‚ü©_ő≤).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(‚ü®A¬∑B ‚ąí B¬∑A‚ü©_ő≤).re = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_ő≤(H_Ising)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsing_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn ő≤ H_Ising).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((‚ü®O‚ü©_ő≤).re : ‚Ąā) = ‚ü®O‚ü©_ő≤` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(ŌĀ_ő≤^n) = Z(nő≤) / Z(ő≤)^n` for the Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_self_eq` | `‚ü®H_Ising‚ü©_ő≤ = -J ¬∑ ‚ąĎ ‚ü®ŌÉ^z ŌÉ^z‚ü©_ő≤ + (-h) ¬∑ ‚ąĎ ‚ü®ŌÉ^x‚ü©_ő≤` (energy as bond + transverse-field decomposition) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki ¬ß3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp ő≤ H` | `exp(-ő≤H) := Matrix.exp (-ő≤ ‚ÄĘ H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-ő≤H)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0¬∑H) = 1` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, pp. 75‚Äď78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add` | `exp(-(ő≤‚āĀ+ő≤‚āā)H) = exp(-ő≤‚āĀH) ¬∑ exp(-ő≤‚āāH)` (one-parameter group) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-ő≤(H‚āĀ+H‚āā)) = exp(-ő≤H‚āĀ) ¬∑ exp(-ő≤H‚āā)` for commuting `H‚āĀ, H‚āā` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_neg_mul_self` | `exp(ő≤H) ¬∑ exp(-ő≤H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_self_mul_neg` | `exp(-ő≤H) ¬∑ exp(ő≤H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isUnit` | `exp(-ő≤H)` is invertible | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_ne_zero` | `exp(-ő≤H) ‚Č† 0` (corollary of `gibbsExp_isUnit`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

| `gibbsState_inv` | `(ŌĀ_ő≤)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ = Z(ő≤) ¬∑ e^{ő≤H}` when `Z(ő≤) ‚Č† 0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_smul_gibbsState_eq_gibbsExp` | `Z(ő≤) ¬∑ ŌĀ_ő≤ = e^{-ő≤H}` when `Z(ő≤) ‚Č† 0` (canonical rescaled identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_mul_gibbsExpectation_eq` | `Z(ő≤) ¬∑ ‚ü®A‚ü©_ő≤ = Tr(e^{-ő≤H} ¬∑ A)` when `Z(ő≤) ‚Č† 0` (canonical unnormalised expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_natCast_mul` | `exp(-(n¬∑ő≤)H) = (exp(-ő≤H))^n` for `n : ‚Ąē` (exact discrete semigroup identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_two_mul` | `exp(-(2ő≤)H) = exp(-ő≤H) ¬∑ exp(-ő≤H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

| `gibbsState_zero_inv` | `ŌĀ_0‚ĀĽ¬Ļ = dim ¬∑ I` (matrix inverse at ő≤ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_zero` | `‚ü®A‚ü©_0 = (1/dim) ¬∑ Tr A` (high-temperature limit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState ő≤ H` | `ŌĀ := (1/Z) ‚ÄĘ exp(-ő≤H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_trace` | `Tr(ŌĀ) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_isHermitian` | `ŌĀ` is Hermitian | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

| `gibbsExpectation_one` | `‚ü®1‚ü© = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_add` | `‚ü®O‚āĀ + O‚āā‚ü© = ‚ü®O‚āĀ‚ü© + ‚ü®O‚āā‚ü©` (linearity in observable) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_smul` | `‚ü®c ¬∑ O‚ü© = c ¬∑ ‚ü®O‚ü©` (scalar linearity, `c : ‚Ąā`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_neg` | `‚ü®-O‚ü© = -‚ü®O‚ü©` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sub` | `‚ü®A - B‚ü© = ‚ü®A‚ü© - ‚ü®B‚ü©` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sum` | `‚ü®‚ąĎ i ‚ąą s, f i‚ü© = ‚ąĎ i ‚ąą s, ‚ü®f i‚ü©` (finite-sum linearity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_commute_hamiltonian` | `[exp(-ő≤H), H] = 0` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

| `gibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(‚ü®H‚ü©_ő≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H` (real energy expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

| `gibbsCovariance ő≤ H A B` | `Cov_ő≤(A, B) := ‚ü®A ¬∑ B‚ü©_ő≤ ‚ąí ‚ü®A‚ü©_ő≤ ¬∑ ‚ü®B‚ü©_ő≤` (canonical-ensemble complex covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

### Heisenberg chain (Tasaki ¬ß3.5)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß3.5, p. 89.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf G J` | the canonical pairwise coupling `őõ ‚Üí őõ ‚Üí ‚Ąā` of a `SimpleGraph G` with uniform edge weight `J`: returns `J` on edges of `G`, zero otherwise (graph-centric bridge) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf_symm` / `_self` / `_real` | symmetry (from `G.Adj` symmetry), vanishing on the diagonal (from irreflexivity), and reality (for real edge weight) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.pathGraph_adj_iff` / `cycleGraph_adj_iff` | path / cycle graph adjacency in the explicit `x.val + 1 = y.val ‚ą® ...` form used elsewhere in the codebase | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+1) ‚Üí Fin (N+1) ‚Üí ‚Ąā`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds, zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+2) ‚Üí Fin (N+2) ‚Üí ‚Ąā`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds (mod N+2), zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the open-chain coupling is `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the periodic-chain coupling is `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_isHermitian_of_real_symm` | for any real symmetric coupling `J` the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ő£_{x,y} J(x,y) Ňú_x ¬∑ Ňú_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_couplingOf_isHermitian` | **graph-centric** Hermiticity: for any `SimpleGraph G` and real edge weight `J : ‚Ąā`, the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` is Hermitian. The chain instances are corollaries via the bridge theorems | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph G J` | named wrapper = `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` (parallel to `isingHamiltonianOnGraph`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | corollaries re-exposed under the named wrapper | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` / `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridges: chain Gibbs = graph Gibbs on pathGraph/cycleGraph | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_eq_isingGibbsStateOnGraph` | chain Ising Gibbs = `isingGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) ő≤ (-J/2) h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | the periodic Ising Gibbs state commutes with the periodic Ising Hamiltonian (free corollary of `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsExpectation_zero` / `_im_of_isHermitian` / `_commutator_hamiltonian` / `_hamiltonian_im` / `_hamiltonian_pow_im` / `isingCycle_partitionFn_im` / `_ofReal_re_eq` / `isingCycleGibbsState_pow_trace` | periodic-Ising expectation companions of the open-chain `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation*` family: ő≤ = 0 closed form, real-valuedness for Hermitian observables, conservation `‚ü®[H, A]‚ü© = 0`, energy / energy-power expectations real, partition-function real, real-cast `((‚ü®O‚ü©_ő≤).re : ‚Ąā) = ‚ü®O‚ü©_ő≤`, R√©nyi-n trace `Tr(ŌĀ_ő≤^n) = Z(nő≤) / Z(ő≤)^n` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph N ő≤ G J U` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Hubbard Hamiltonian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity / commute corollaries | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |

| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.boxProd_decidableAdj` | `DecidableRel (G ‚Ė° H).Adj` for the box product (mathlib provides only the bare definition); enables 2D / nD lattices via `couplingOf` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerChainGraph` | the **infinite** one-dimensional chain `‚Ą§` as a `SimpleGraph` (= `hasse ‚Ą§`), the infinite-volume analogue of `pathGraph (N+1)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerChainGraph_adj_iff` | adjacency: `a ~ b ‚ÜĒ b = a + 1 ‚ą® a = b + 1` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerSquareLatticeGraph` | the **infinite** 2D square lattice on `‚Ą§ √ó ‚Ą§` as `integerChainGraph ‚Ė° integerChainGraph`; infinite analogue of `squareLatticeCoupling` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerSquareLatticeGraph_adj_iff` | adjacency: nearest neighbour in one coordinate, equal in the other | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |

| `cubicLatticeCoupling N J`, `cubicLatticeHeisenberg_isHermitian` | the 3D open-boundary cubic lattice on `Fin (N+1)^3` realised as `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1) ‚Ė° pathGraph (N+1) ‚Ė° pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)`; Hermiticity is a one-line corollary | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareLatticeHeisenbergGibbsState` / `_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Gibbs state of the 2D open-boundary square-lattice Heisenberg Hamiltonian + Hermiticity + commute pair | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareTorusHeisenbergGibbsState` / `_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Gibbs state of the 2D torus Heisenberg Hamiltonian + companions | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `cubicLatticeHeisenbergGibbsState` / `_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Gibbs state of the 3D open-boundary cubic-lattice Heisenberg Hamiltonian + companions | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareLatticeHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` / `squareLatticeHeisenberg_partitionFn_im` | 2D open square-lattice Heisenberg energy expectation real / partition function real | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareTorusHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` / `squareTorusHeisenberg_partitionFn_im` | 2D torus Heisenberg energy expectation real / partition function real | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` | for `N = 1` (the 2-site open chain on `Fin 2`), `H_open = -2J ¬∑ spinHalfDot 0 1` (explicit one-bond reduction; Tasaki ¬ß2.4 simplest concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=1) ¬∑ |‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ‚ü© = -(J/2) ¬∑ |‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ `|‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ‚ü©` lies in the `S = 1` triplet sector and is an exact eigenvector with eigenvalue `-J/2` (this is the ferromagnetic ground state for `J < 0`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_singlet` | `H_open(N=1) ¬∑ (|‚ÜĎ‚Üď‚ü© - |‚Üď‚ÜĎ‚ü©) = (3J/2) ¬∑ (|‚ÜĎ‚Üď‚ü© - |‚Üď‚ÜĎ‚ü©)` ‚ÄĒ singlet eigenvalue, the antiferromagnetic ground state for `J > 0` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `H_open(N=1) ¬∑ |‚Üď‚Üď‚ü© = -(J/2) ¬∑ |‚Üď‚Üď‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ all-down state has the same eigenvalue as all-up (both are `S = 1` triplet states) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_triplet_zero` | `H_open(N=1) ¬∑ (|‚ÜĎ‚Üď‚ü© + |‚Üď‚ÜĎ‚ü©) = -(J/2) ¬∑ (|‚ÜĎ‚Üď‚ü© + |‚Üď‚ÜĎ‚ü©)` ‚ÄĒ triplet `m = 0` state, completing the 3-fold degenerate triplet representation `S = 1` with eigenvalue `-J/2` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_eq` | for `N = 2` (the 3-site open chain on `Fin 3`, 2 bonds), `H_open = -2J ¬∑ (spinHalfDot 0 1 + spinHalfDot 1 2)` ‚ÄĒ explicit two-bond reduction | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) ¬∑ |‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ‚ü© = -J ¬∑ |‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ confirming the linear scaling `E(|‚ÜĎ..‚ÜĎ‚ü©) = -N¬∑J/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : ‚Ąē`, `ő£_{x,y ‚ąą Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N ¬∑ J : ‚Ąā)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | for any `N : ‚Ąē`, `J : ‚ĄĚ`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H_open ¬∑ |s..s‚ü© = -(N¬∑J/2 : ‚Ąā) ¬∑ |s..s‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ both `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) share the same eigenvalue by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|¬∑S¬≤` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(N¬∑J/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : ‚Ąē`, `J : ‚ĄĚ`, `k : ‚Ąē`, `H_open ¬∑ ((Ňútot^-)^k ¬∑ |‚ÜĎ..‚ÜĎ‚ü©) = -(N¬∑J/2 : ‚Ąā) ¬∑ ((Ňútot^-)^k ¬∑ |‚ÜĎ..‚ÜĎ‚ü©)` ‚ÄĒ the unnormalised Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|ő¶_M‚ü©` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|‚Üď..‚Üď‚ü©`, same eigenvalue `-(N¬∑J/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_open` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_periodic` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs A‚ÄďC).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal ‚Üí primitive (Seneta ¬ß1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ¬ß1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) ‚Č§ (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)¬∑x ‚Č§ Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ¬ß1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r¬∑v`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` ‚üĻ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = őľv`, `v ‚Č• 0`, `v ‚Č† 0` ‚üĻ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg ‚üĻ ‚ąÉ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ¬ß1.2, pp. 27‚Äď28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg ‚üĻ ‚ąÉ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ¬ß1.2, pp. 27‚Äď28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_nonneg_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry**, retained for docs) symmetric nonneg max eigenvalue has nonneg eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian ‚üĻ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `őľ` ‚üĻ every real eigenvector at `őľ` is `s ‚ÄĘ v` for some scalar `s` (the `őľ`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t ‚ÄĘ u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `őľ`, hence `‚ąĚ v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u ‚ąĚ v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ¬ß1.2 (pp. 27‚Äď28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` Marshall‚ÄďLieb‚ÄďMattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> ‚ÄĒ the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(Ňú‚ĀĽ_tot)^M|all-up‚ü©` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0‚Üí1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector Perron‚ÄďFrobenius ground state
> (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[ń§, Ňú‚ĀĽ_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { ŌÉ : V ‚Üí Fin (N + 1) // magSumS ŌÉ = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ő¶` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `őľ` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `ő≥` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card¬∑N/2‚ąíM)(card¬∑N/2‚ąíM‚ąí1)`, then `Ňú‚ĀĽ_tot ¬∑ magSectorEmbedding ő¶` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `őľ` (via `[ń§, Ňú‚ĀĽ_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector Perron‚ÄďFrobenius ground states (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `Ňú‚Āļ_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card¬∑N/2‚ąíK)(card¬∑N/2‚ąíK+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `Ňú_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ŌÉ = ‚ąĎ_{x‚ąąA}(N/2‚ąíŌÉ_x)`. Submodule API (zero/add/smul/neg/sub/finite-sum membership), disjointness of distinct eigenvalues, and basis-state membership. Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(Ňú_A)¬≤`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(Ňú_A)¬≤`/`(Ňú_¬¨A)¬≤` eigenvalues `ő≥_A, ő≥_B` obey `ő≥_A.re ‚Č§ s_A(s_A+1)`, `ő≥_B.re ‚Č§ s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToySublatticeBounds.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|¬¨A|)¬∑N`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|¬∑N/2 ‚ąí M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A ‚ąí s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|¬¨A|)¬∑N`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `őľ ‚Č§ predicted ‚ąí s_A(s_A+1) ‚ąí s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(Ňú_tot)¬≤`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness ‚ÄĒ exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `hőľ` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` | **Toy ground energy is minimal in its sector**: the Marshall-positive toy ground state (energy `őľ`) has `őľ ‚Č§ E` for every sector eigenvector `ŌÜ ‚Č† 0` of the toy Heisenberg sector matrix at real energy `E`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyGroundEnergyBound.lean`) |

A two-sided squeeze (magnetization lower bound #3675 + sublattice upper bounds #3672 + energy formula #3673), isolating the extremal-sector and reference-energy obligations as hypotheses; no Clebsch‚ÄďGordan (Issue #3674, PR 2). From the sublattice Cartan identity `Ňú_A^- Ňú_A^+ = (Ňú_A^1)¬≤+(Ňú_A^2)¬≤‚ąíŇú_A^(3)` (so `(Ňú_A)¬≤ = Ňú_A^-Ňú_A^+ + Ňú_A^(3) + (Ňú_A^(3))¬≤`); on a highest-weight `Ňú_A^(3)`-eigenvector the `Ňú_A^-Ňú_A^+` term vanishes. | `toy_joint_eigenvector_energy_re_ge` | **Toy minimum-energy bound `ń§_toy ‚Č• E`** (option (a) ‚ÄĒ coupled bound #3723 + arithmetic #3716): a joint `(Ňú_tot)¬≤`/`(Ňú_A)¬≤`/`(Ňú_¬¨A)¬≤`-eigenvector `Ōą` (eigenvalues `ő≥_tot, ő≥_A, ő≥_B`) in a magnetization level (with `|¬¨A| ‚Č§ |A|`) has toy energy `(ő≥_tot ‚ąí ő≥_A ‚ąí ő≥_B).re ‚Č• E = (s_A‚ąís_B)(s_A‚ąís_B+1) ‚ąí s_A(s_A+1) ‚ąí s_B(s_B+1)`. Writing the (non-negative #3724, `‚Č§ s(s+1)` #3672) sublattice Casimir eigenvalues as `a(a+1)`, `b(b+1)` (real spins via the square root), the coupled bound (#3723) gives `ő≥_tot.re ‚Č• |a‚ąíb|(|a‚ąíb|+1)`, hence `(ő≥_tot‚ąíő≥_A‚ąíő≥_B).re ‚Č• |a‚ąíb|(|a‚ąíb|+1) ‚ąí a(a+1) ‚ąí b(b+1) = f(a,b) ‚Č• f(s_A,s_B) = E` (#3716). This is the interior-sector toy minimum-energy bound (pins `r_min(M) = E` with the predicted-energy witness #3715) (Issue #3542). | `tasaki23_toy_min_energy_arith_eq` | **Strict (equality) arithmetic core** (option (a)): if `f(a,b) = f(s_A,s_B)` on the box `0 ‚Č§ a ‚Č§ s_A`, `0 ‚Č§ b ‚Č§ s_B ‚Č§ s_A` with `s_B > 0`, then `(a,b) = (s_A,s_B)` (the minimiser of `f(a,b) = |a‚ąíb|(|a‚ąíb|+1) ‚ąí a(a+1) ‚ąí b(b+1)` is the corner). When the toy energy attains its minimum `E` the toy-minimum-energy chain is tight, so this pins the toy ground state's sublattice spins to the maximal `(s_A,s_B)`, hence its total Casimir to the predicted value (Issue #3542). This is the SU(2) non-vanishing input for the inward-ladder discharge of the non-admissible-sector lower bound: an eigenvector outside the band can be moved to the band edge by `Ňú‚ąď_tot` without annihilation (Issue #3542). | `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 truly-unconditional capstone for the bipartite toy coupling** (#3887 extension chain, Thm23-#3887.12‚Äď.21, PR #3891): the canonical `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` API is now the structural statement, dropping the vacuous-at-`N=1` `h_intermediate` hypothesis (`‚ąÄ ŌĄ, ‚ąÄ x, ‚ąÉ z, A z ‚Č† A x ‚ąß (ŌĄ z).val < N`; unsatisfiable when `ŌĄ ‚Č° Fin.last 1`). At spin-1/2 (N=1) this is the **first truly-unconditional Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 closure**. The already-`h_intermediate`-free pieces (`tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common`, `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness`, `exists_predictedEnergy_sector_eigenvector_of_mem`) are reused directly. | `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 truly-unconditional capstone for general bipartite J** (PR #3893, Issue #3892): the canonical general-`J` closure proves `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3 A N J c` for ANY real symmetric non-negative bipartite coupling `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph at any `N ‚Č• 1`, under canonical orientation `|¬¨A| ‚Č§ |A|` with `s_B > 0`. This removes BOTH the toy-coupling restriction of PR #3891 and the vacuous-at-N=1 `h_intermediate` restriction of the older #3738 capstone. At spin-1/2 (N=1) this is the **first truly-unconditional Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 closure for general bipartite J**. Chain: structural sector lift + Casimir (Step 1), structural adjacent-sector common-energy step (Step 2), structural common ground-state energy (Step 3, TIER 4 constancy), capstone (Step 4) combining Step 3 with PR #3891's `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence` and the already-`h_intermediate`-free `tasaki23_general_hOutside` + `tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` + `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive`. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_unique_groundState_has_zero_magnetization` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 partial conclusion: unique GS has zero `Ňú¬≥_tot` magnetization** (Issue #3739, PR #3896): if the anisotropic Heisenberg ground-state eigenspace has `finrank ‚Ąā ‚Č§ 1`, every non-zero ground state `ő¶` satisfies `Ňú¬≥_tot ő¶ = 0`. Proof: ő¶, Ňú¬≥_tot ő¶, őė ő¶ all lie in the GS eigenspace (`H` commutes with `Ňú¬≥_tot` (#3741) and with the reflection `őė` (#3745)). From `finrank ‚Č§ 1` + ő¶ ‚Č† 0 and `Module.finrank_le_one_iff`, `Ňú¬≥_tot ő¶ = ő≥ ő¶` and `őė ő¶ = őī ő¶` for some scalars. `őė¬≤ = 1` ‚üĻ `őī¬≤ = 1` ‚üĻ `őī ‚Č† 0`. Apply the anticommutation `őė Ňú¬≥_tot = -Ňú¬≥_tot őė` (#3744) to ő¶: get `őīő≥ ‚ÄĘ ő¶ = -ő≥őī ‚ÄĘ ő¶` ‚üĻ `2őīő≥ ‚ÄĘ ő¶ = 0` ‚üĻ `őīő≥ = 0` ‚üĻ `ő≥ = 0` (since őī ‚Č† 0). This is the second conclusion of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 once uniqueness is established (uniqueness itself awaits the deformation argument from the SU(2) point, separate forthcoming work). | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_at_SU2_eigenspace_eq_heisenbergHamiltonianS` / `heisenbergHamiltonianS_unique_groundState_has_zero_magnetization` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 SU(2) base case via Theorem 2.3 reduction** (Issue #3739, PR #3897): at the SU(2) point `(őĽ=1, D=0)`, the anisotropic Hamiltonian reduces to the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian (via `anisotropicHeisenbergS_one_zero`), so every eigenspace coincides. The first lemma gives the direct eigenspace identification; the second specializes PR #3896 to the SU(2) point, giving `Ňú¬≥_tot|ő¶‚ü©=0` for any non-zero GS of the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian under the GS uniqueness hypothesis. Prepares the SU(2) base case for the deformation argument: once uniqueness is established at the SU(2) point (for symmetric `|A|=|¬¨A|` via Theorem 2.2 + SU(2) singlet structure, separate forthcoming work), the `Ňú¬≥_tot|ő¶‚ü©=0` conclusion follows immediately. Proof: evaluate `H ¬∑ Ňú¬≥_tot = Ňú¬≥_tot ¬∑ H` at `(ŌÉ, ŌĄ)`; since `Ňú¬≥_tot` is diagonal with entries `magEigenvalueS`, get `H ŌÉ ŌĄ ¬∑ M_ŌĄ = M_ŌÉ ¬∑ H ŌÉ ŌĄ`. When `magSumS ŌÉ ‚Č† magSumS ŌĄ`, Nat-cast injectivity gives `magEigenvalueS ŌÉ ‚Č† magEigenvalueS ŌĄ`, so `H ŌÉ ŌĄ = 0`. This enables sector-by-sector spectral decomposition of any anisotropic H-eigenvector, key building block toward Theorem 2.4 obligation (2.a) uniqueness via the sector-decomposition + reflection argument. Direct consequence of `magSubspaceS_disjoint` (existing): for `a ‚ÄĘ ő¶‚āĀ + b ‚ÄĘ ő¶‚āā = 0`, get `a ‚ÄĘ ő¶‚āĀ ‚ąą magSubspaceS őõ N M‚āĀ ‚ą© magSubspaceS őõ N M‚āā = ‚ä•`, so `a ‚ÄĘ ő¶‚āĀ = 0`, giving `a = 0` (since ő¶‚āĀ ‚Č† 0), and similarly `b = 0`. Proof: PR #3902 gives `{ő¶, ő®, őė ő®}` linearly independent; `őė ő®` is also at energy őľ via őė commutes with H (#3745); the 3-LI family lifts to the eigenspace `‚Ü•E` via `LinearIndependent.of_comp E.subtype`; `LinearIndependent.fintype_card_le_finrank` gives `3 ‚Č§ finrank ‚Ąā ‚Ü•E`, contradicting `finrank ‚Č§ 2` via `omega`. This is the direct contradiction in the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ‚Č§ 1` argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24FinrankLeTwoContradiction.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigvec_nonadmis_projection_zero` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 eigvec non-admissible projection = 0** (Issue #3739, PR #3904): under `finrank ‚Č§ 2` at `őľ` + admissible eigvec exists, any eigvec ő® at őľ has zero `magSectorEmbedding (magSectorRestriction (M := M) ő®)` for every non-admissible sector index `M` (with `2M ‚Č† |őõ|¬∑N`). Proof: the projection is an eigvec at őľ via PR #3899 and lies in `magSubspaceS őõ N M'` with `M' = |őõ|¬∑N/2 - M ‚Č† 0` (non-admissible Ňú¬≥_tot eigenvalue). If non-zero, PR #3903 derives False; hence the projection is `0`. Penultimate step toward the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ‚Č§ 1`: combined with `eq_sum_magSectorEmbedding_magSectorRestriction`, the eigenspace at őľ is contained in the admissible sector. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24EigvecNonadmisProjectionZero.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigvec_in_admis_sector_of_finrank_le_two` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 eigvec at őľ ‚ąą admissible sector** (Issue #3739, PR #3905): under `finrank ‚Č§ 2` at `őľ` + admissible eigvec exists, any eigvec ő® at őľ lies in `magSubspaceS őõ N 0` (the `Ňú¬≥_tot = 0` admissible sector). Proof: decompose ő® via `eq_sum_magSectorEmbedding_magSectorRestriction`; each summand at non-admissible M is `0` by PR #3904; each summand at admissible M (where `2M = |őõ|¬∑N`) lies in `magSubspaceS őõ N (|őõ|¬∑N/2 - M) = magSubspaceS őõ N 0`; sum of submodule members is in the submodule. Final piece toward the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ‚Č§ 1` argument: combined with within-admissible-sector PF `finrank = 1`, the full Hilbert space finrank at őľ is `‚Č§ 1`. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_finrank_le_one_from_admis_pf` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 `finrank ‚Č§ 1` from admis. PF + `finrank ‚Č§ 2`** (Issue #3739, PR #3906): if the anisotropic Hamiltonian eigenspace at őľ has `finrank ‚Č§ 2` AND an admissible eigvec exists at őľ AND the within-admissible-sector eigenspace at őľ has `finrank ‚Č§ 1`, then the full Hilbert space eigenspace at őľ has `finrank ‚Č§ 1`. Proof: by PR #3905, the eigenspace `E` is contained in the admissible submodule `A`, so `E ‚äď A = E`; the within-admissible hypothesis gives `finrank (E ‚äď A) ‚Č§ 1`; substitution gives `finrank E ‚Č§ 1`. Together with `őė Ňú¬≥_tot őė = ‚ąíŇú¬≥_tot` (#3744) this completes the `M ‚ÜĒ ‚ąíM` reflection symmetry of the Mattis‚ÄďNishimori argument. | `matrix_similar_eigenspace_inter_finrank_eq` | **(g.3) similarity-invariant intersection eigenspace finrank** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3835): generalises `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq` (#3746 family) to intersections with a second operator's eigenspace, provided the similarity `U` commutes with that operator `Q`. Given `U * Uinv = 1`, `Uinv * U = 1`, `H' = Uinv * H * U`, AND `Q * U = U * Q`, then `finrank ‚Ąā (eig H' őľ ‚äď ker(Q=c)) = finrank ‚Ąā (eig H őľ ‚äď ker(Q=c))`. The core technical step for transferring per-parity-block PF bounds from the dressed `ń§'_dressed` to the bare `ń§'`, since the Marshall sign diagonal `őė_A` commutes with the diagonal `magParityDiagS P`. Result for `p < 2`: `‚ąÉ őĹ : ‚ĄĚ, finrank ‚Ąā (eig (axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS J lam D N) (őĹ : ‚Ąā) ‚äď ker(P=(-1)^p)) ‚Č§ 1`. This **closes the bare `ń§` ‚Č§ 2 link in the (g) chain**, conditional on per-parity-block PF hypotheses (discharged separately per sector by #3836). The remaining gap to unconditional obligation (1) is the őĹ-sector matching (PF eigenvalues of even/odd sectors coinciding at the ground-state energy), which is mathematically distinct (Hermitian spectral minimisation) and deferred. Combined with the forward direction (#3832), gives the finrank equality `finrank ‚Ąā (eig M.submatrix őĹ) = finrank ‚Ąā (eig M őĹ ‚äď ker(P=(-1)^p))` for `p < 2`. The equality unlocks őĹ-sector matching arguments: per-block submatrix eig finrank equals the full eig ‚äď ker(P) finrank, so per-sector PF simplicity (#3834/#3836) directly transfers to constraints on the FULL eigenspace at that energy. | `eigenspace_finrank_eq_of_commuting_involution` | **(h.2) involution eigenspace decomposition ‚ÄĒ finrank EQUALITY** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3841): strengthens `eigenspace_finrank_le_of_commuting_involution` (#3776) from `‚Č§` to `=`. For finite-dim `T, P` with `T‚ąėP = P‚ąėT` and `P‚ąėP = id`, the `T`-eigenspace at any `őľ` splits as a **direct sum** across the two `P`-eigenspaces `¬Ī1`. The `‚Č§` was tight because the two intersected `¬Ī1` eigenspaces of `P` are disjoint (a single eigenvector cannot have two distinct `P`-eigenvalues), so `Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` collapses to a sum (inf = 0). | `matrix_eigenspace_finrank_eq_sum_parity_blocks` (with `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`, `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`) | **(h.3) block-sum finrank equality** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3842): combines (h.1) #3840 and (h.2) #3841 to give the block-sum equality for any parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `magParityDiagS`: `finrank ‚Ąā (eig M őľ) = finrank ‚Ąā (eig M.submatrix_0 őľ) + finrank ‚Ąā (eig M.submatrix_1 őľ)`. Central tool for unconditional `‚Č§ 2` at any őľ where both per-block bounds hold simultaneously. Combined with PF (per-sector simplicity at the per-sector spectrum minimum) and a Hermitian spectral identification of the GS energy as `min(őĹ_0, őĹ_1)`, gives the unconditional bare `ń§'` GS degeneracy `‚Č§ 2`. Fix: in the same-sublattice over/under hard case there is an *alternative path* requiring `ŌÉ z > 0` instead of `ŌÉ z < N`; at `N ‚Č• 1` every `(ŌÉ z).val` satisfies at least one of the two. Structural branching variant drops `h_intermediate` entirely, requiring only `hA_ne + hB_ne + 1 ‚Č§ N`. | `axisSwapUnitarySSpinS_anisotropic_axisSwapped_eigenspace_finrank_eq` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general` | **General spin-S axis-swap discharge for Theorem 2.4 obligation (1)** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): the concrete `ŌÄ/2` rotation about spin-axis 1, `axisSwapUnitarySSpinS N`, now instantiates the earlier `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` interface for every spin parameter `N`. The PR exposes no-argument wrappers for the axis-swap eigenspace-dimension equality, the conditional parity-block and submatrix-block `‚Č§ 2` bounds, the min-block `‚Č§ 2` bound, the Hermitian-minimum equality, and the structural general-N capstone `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general`. This discharges the explicit axis-swap-unitary argument in the strict case-(i) degeneracy chain; the SU(2)-uniqueness target wrappers are handled in the later obligation-(2) row, and Tasaki case (ii) now has conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges recorded in the case-(ii) rows, with the strict-gap derivation still explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43‚Äď44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AxisSwapUnitarySSpinS.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_balanced_sector_pf_at_target` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S target conditional bridge with balanced-sector PF discharged** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): packages the final linear-algebra step after the general spin-S `finrank ‚Č§ 2` degeneracy capstone. If the target full ground eigenspace has `finrank ‚Č§ 2`, the balanced sector attains the full ground energy, and the balanced-sector ground eigenspace has `finrank ‚Č§ 1`, then the full target ground eigenspace has `finrank ‚Č§ 1`; the companion wrapper then gives zero total `Ňú¬≥` magnetization for every non-zero target ground state. The new general spin-S PF wrapper chooses a finite strict diagonal shift and applies the existing anisotropic sector PF/minimum theorem, so the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit balanced-sector PF callback. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input is superseded by the strict-gap wrappers in the next row; for case (ii), the conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges are recorded in the case-(ii) rows and the strict-gap derivation remains explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43‚Äď44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetConditionalBridge.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSBalancedSectorPFTarget.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergParametricPath_in_case_ii_region` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S case-(ii) path and conditional target bridge** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that the deformation path from `(1,0)` to a target satisfying `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` remains in the case-(ii) region on `[0,1]`, then repackages the balanced-sector PF target bridge under case-(ii) hypotheses. These first case-(ii) wrappers keep the remaining target inputs explicit as balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2`; they do not reuse the case-(i) axis-swap sign argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIConditionalBridge.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from strict sector gap** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): composes the arbitrary-parameter strict-gap target bridge with the case-(ii) theorem surface. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input from the first case-(ii) wrappers is replaced by the strict gap `E_balanced < E_M` over every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector under `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0`; the remaining full ground eigenspace `finrank <= 2` input stays explicit in this intermediate wrapper. This is a case-(ii) bridge step, not the final case-(ii) endpoint. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIStrictGapBridge.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_groundState_mem_balanced_sector_of_strict_gap` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` | **General spin-S target bridge from strict sector gap without the full `finrank <= 2` input** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that if the balanced sector attains the full ground energy and every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector has strictly larger minimum, then every full ground vector lies in the balanced magnetization sector. This uses the sector-projection eigenvector bridge and `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min`, not the case-(i) parity-block `finrank <= 2` contradiction. Composing this containment with balanced-sector Perron--Frobenius simplicity gives full target uniqueness and zero total magnetization from strict sector gap alone; the case-(ii)-named wrappers keep only the theorem-facing `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` hypotheses plus the strict-gap input. This removes the explicit case-(ii) full `finrank <= 2` input; deriving the case-(ii) strict gap remains future work. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetFromStrictGapNoFullFinrank.lean`) |

The symmetric `|A| = |¬¨A|` Theorem 2.3 sector band is reduced to a singleton, the predicted spin and Casimir collapse to `0`, the balanced PF lift is specialized to a total-Casimir-zero vector, the Theorem 2.3 common energy is identified with the full Heisenberg Hermitian minimum, and `finrank ‚Č§ 1` is transferred from the balanced sector matrix to the full eigenspace once outside-sector projections are excluded. A strict outside-sector lower-bound callback now implies those outside projections vanish and hence gives the full `finrank ‚Č§ 1` bridge; the packaged endpoint combines Theorem 2.3, balanced-cardinality arithmetic, sector PF simplicity, and strict outside-sector ordering into a direct full SU(2) uniqueness conclusion. The remaining mathematical task is to prove that strict outside-sector lower bound from the MLM/Casimir chain. This is the equality-case bridge needed for the MLM/Casimir strict outside-sector proof: once the outside-sector ladder lands in the singleton balanced sector at the common energy, sector PF simplicity pins the landed vector to the zero-Casimir balanced PF line. The hypothesis bundle and conclusion match the per-sector bundled Theorem 2.2 `marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full` (#869) exactly ‚ÄĒ real symmetric coupling (`(J x y).im = 0`, `star (J x y) = J x y`, `J x y = J y x`, `0 ‚Č§ (J x y).re`), bipartite support, positivity on `bipartiteCompleteGraphOf A`, non-empty sublattices, a spectral shift `c` strictly above the dressed diagonal, the #869 intermediate-existence hypothesis, plus sector non-emptiness ‚ÄĒ and asserts existence of a common GS energy `őľ` realised on every admissible sector `M ‚ąą tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N` by a Marshall-positive eigenvector (Tasaki (2.5.4) with `ŌÉ = M`), with within-sector uniqueness up to positive scalar, plus global minimality of `őľ`. Together with `tasaki23PredictedDegeneracy A N = \|\|A\| ‚ąí \|¬¨A\|\|¬∑N + 1 = 2 S_tot + 1` (cardinality of `tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`), this packages the predicted total spin `S_tot = \|\|A\| ‚ąí \|¬¨A\|\|¬∑N/2` and the predicted `2 S_tot + 1` degeneracy. Proof: iterate #869 across `tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`. These are the finite-range bookkeeping lemmas used to iterate the lowering/raising adjacent-sector energy comparisons across the Theorem 2.3 multiplet. If `M` is admissible and strictly before the right endpoint, the real lowering-kernel endpoint value for `m = \|V\|N/2 - M` is strictly below the predicted value, hence not equal after coercion to `‚Ąā`; dually, if `M+1` is admissible and strictly above the left endpoint, the raising-kernel endpoint value is strictly below the predicted value and therefore not equal. | `tasaki23_signed_single_site_lowering_component_nonneg_of_A_false` / `tasaki23_signed_single_site_lowering_component_nonpos_of_A_true` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_offA_sum_nonneg` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_onA_sum_nonpos` / `tasaki23_exists_lowerable_offA_of_offA_occupation_pos` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_offA_sum_pos_of_exists_lowerable_offA` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_offA_sum_pos_of_offA_occupation_pos` | **Boundary-inclusive lowered local sign bounds and sublattice filtered sums for Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the strict local lowering split is extended across the zero-target boundary. Summing over the filtered finite sets gives a non-negative off-`A` signed sum and a non-positive on-`A` signed sum. Positive off-`A` occupation produces an actually lowerable off-`A` witness, and either that witness or the aggregate occupation hypothesis gives strict positivity of the off-`A` filtered sum by `Finset.sum_pos'`. These are decomposition bounds for the remaining strict lowered site-sum positivity proof, not yet the dominance argument for the full sum. Under source-sector Marshall positivity, the signed real contribution is strictly positive for `A x = false` and strictly negative for `A x = true`, matching the same one-site Marshall sign product as the lowering predecessor case. Positive off-`A` vacancy produces an actually raiseable off-`A` witness, and either that witness or the aggregate vacancy hypothesis gives strict positivity of the off-`A` filtered sum by `Finset.sum_pos'`. These are the raising-direction decomposition bounds for the remaining strict raised site-sum positivity proof.
